name: Rocket Pudding Tools
description: A growing registry of self-contained web tools.

tools.name: DAG Layout Lab
tools.tagline: Prototype & benchmark DAG layout algorithms on an Excalidraw-style canvas.
tools.description: Interactive lab for comparing directed-acyclic-graph layout algorithms — layered/Sugiyama with barycenter crossing reduction, Coffman-Graham, and rank-weighted stress — with a live benchmark leaderboard, node metadata cards, mermaid import, grouped containers, Caramel-URI provenance labels, and SVG/PNG/PDF/HTML export.

provenance.note: designed in Claude Design; built here

tools.name: VizKit Gallery
tools.tagline: Dependency-free Canvas2D chart widgets — one contract, 36-chart gallery.
tools.description: Performance-first, zero-dependency data-visualization primitives: 20 composable marks, a Canvas2D core, and framework-agnostic <viz-widget> custom elements. The gallery renders 36 chart types across 19 taxonomy categories on one shared contract, in the Slate Console theme. MIT.

tools.name: Mermaid Studio
tools.tagline: Live Mermaid diagram editor with SVG/PNG export
tools.description: Type Mermaid source on the left and watch the diagram render live on the right (debounced, parse errors shown inline while the last good render stays put). Ships seven example diagrams — flowchart, sequence, class, state, gantt, pie, mindmap — plus copy-source, zoom/fit controls, a resizable split, dark mode, and one-click SVG or PNG export. Mermaid v11.16.0 vendored locally; everything renders in-browser and nothing leaves the page.

tools.name: JSON ⇄ YAML
tools.tagline: Two-pane JSON/YAML converter with live validation
tools.description: Bidirectional JSON ⇄ YAML converter and validator. Type or paste in either pane and the other converts live (debounced); parse errors show precise line/column under the offending pane without clobbering the other side. Per-pane Copy, Pretty (2-space), Minify (JSON), and Clear, plus pane swap, a sample loader, and byte/line counts. Powered by a locally vendored js-yaml v4 — everything runs in the browser, nothing is uploaded.

tools.name: Cron Explainer
tools.tagline: Cron expressions in plain English with next 10 runs
tools.description: Paste a 5- or 6-field cron expression and get a live plain-English sentence (cronstrue), a per-field breakdown chip row with parsed meanings, and the next 10 run times in both local time (Mountain Time labeled by zone) and UTC (croner). Includes preset buttons for common schedules and inline parse errors. Fully self-contained: both MIT libraries vendored locally, only external load is Google Fonts.

tools.name: JWT Inspector
tools.tagline: Decode, humanize, and time-check JSON Web Tokens — entirely in your browser.
tools.description: Paste a JWT to see color-coded header, payload, and signature sections with syntax-highlighted JSON, a registered-claims table that renders exp/iat/nbf as absolute local time plus live-ticking relative countdowns, and a big VALID WINDOW / EXPIRED / NOT YET VALID status pill. Optionally verify HS256 signatures with WebCrypto by pasting the shared secret. Hand-rolled base64url decoding, graceful per-segment error handling, built-in sample token — nothing ever leaves your browser.

tools.name: CSV Workbench
tools.tagline: Paste or drop a CSV — explore, filter, sort, convert.
tools.description: Self-contained CSV explorer: paste, drop, or pick a file and PapaParse handles parsing with delimiter auto-detect and a header-row auto-detect toggle. Sortable 500-row preview with a global filter, per-column stats (type guess, distinct, nulls, min/max/mean), parse-error list, and copy/download export to JSON, NDJSON, or Markdown table respecting the current filter and sort. Sample CSV loader included; runs entirely offline except Google Fonts.

tools.name: QR Forge
tools.tagline: Text and URLs to QR codes with real controls
tools.description: Live QR code generator: type text, a URL, Wi-Fi credentials, or a vCard and watch the code render as you type. Full control over error-correction level (L/M/Q/H), module size, quiet zone, type number, and dark/light module colors. Shows module count, version, and byte length. Export as PNG or matrix-built SVG, or copy the PNG straight to the clipboard. Powered by a locally vendored qrcode-generator v1.5.2 (MIT, Kazuhiko Arase) — no CDN calls at runtime.

tools.name: Markdown Studio
tools.tagline: Split-pane live markdown editor with sandboxed GFM preview.
tools.description: Write GitHub-Flavored Markdown on the left and watch it render instantly on the right — inside a fully sandboxed iframe so pasted HTML and scripts stay inert. Insert-at-cursor formatting toolbar with undo support, proportional synced scrolling, word/char/reading-time stats, localStorage draft autosave, and one-click export to .md, standalone styled .html, or clipboard HTML. Powered by a locally vendored marked v15.

tools.name: Nomnoml Studio
tools.tagline: Live UML and box-diagram editor with crisp PNG/SVG export
tools.description: Type nomnoml source on the left and watch a devicePixelRatio-crisp canvas render live on the right, with inline parse errors that keep the last good drawing. Ships five starter examples (class, flow, state machine, components, mind map), a precise directives cheat sheet, copy-source, and one-click PNG/SVG downloads. Fully self-contained: nomnoml 1.7.0 and its graphre layout engine vendored locally under MIT.

tools.name: AST Explorer
tools.tagline: Paste code, walk its syntax tree
tools.description: Interactive syntax-tree explorer powered by tree-sitter compiled to WebAssembly. Parses JavaScript, Python, and C entirely in the browser with vendored grammar wasms; collapsible named-node tree with positions, click-to-highlight source spans, hover snippets, node-type filtering, parse-time and node-count stats, and copy-tree-as-JSON.

tools.name: PDG Viewer
tools.tagline: Program-dependence graph for JavaScript, client-side
tools.description: Paste a JavaScript function and get a statement-level program-dependence graph as a layered SVG DAG: control dependence solid, data dependence dashed and labeled by variable, via a hand-rolled reaching-definitions walk with loop-carried def-use. Click to trace deps, toggle layers, export PNG or Graphviz DOT. Parsing by vendored acorn v8; honest approximation notes included.

tools.name: CPG Lite
tools.tagline: Joern-style code property graph for one JavaScript function
tools.description: Explore a function's code property graph in the browser: basic blocks with three overlay layers on one node set — CFG edges with T/F branch labels and loop back-edges, a per-variable reaching-definitions DFG, and AST containment. Blocks link back to source lines; layers toggle independently; exports PNG and JSON. acorn-parsed, approximations documented.

tools.name: Import Graph
tools.tagline: Madge-style module dependency graphs, fully in the browser
tools.description: Drop a repo .zip or paste files to get a layered import graph: JS/TS and Python extraction, relative-path resolution, internal modules as cards with LOC and fan-in/out, externals as dimmed pills, and Tarjan-detected circular dependencies drawn red with a cycle list. Search, trace, and export PNG, JSON, or DOT. Unzipping via vendored fflate; nothing leaves the browser.

tools.name: SQLite Workbench
tools.tagline: Open, query, and export SQLite databases entirely in your browser.
tools.description: A zero-backend SQLite client powered by sql.js WASM. Drop in a .sqlite/.db file, start a new database, or load a built-in sample; browse the schema tree, run multi-statement SQL with Ctrl+Enter, sort results, track query history, and export results as CSV/JSON or the whole database as a .sqlite file. No data ever leaves your machine.

tools.name: Graphviz Studio
tools.tagline: Live DOT to SVG with real Graphviz layouts, fully offline
tools.description: A live DOT editor backed by genuine Graphviz compiled to WebAssembly: debounced SVG rendering with zoom/fit/pan, five layout engines (dot, neato, fdp, circo, twopi), bundled examples, inline parse errors that keep the last good render, and SVG/PNG export. The wasm binary is embedded in the vendored bundle — nothing loads from a CDN.

provenance.note: Graphviz is EPL, compiled to WASM

tools.name: esbuild Playground
tools.tagline: Transform and minify TS, JSX, and JS in your browser with esbuild-wasm.
tools.description: In-browser esbuild transform playground: paste TypeScript/TSX/JSX/JS and get live output with control over loader, target, format, JSX mode, granular minify toggles, and sourcemaps. Stats bar shows input vs output bytes and a gzip estimate; errors render with line:column. Fully client-side via vendored esbuild-wasm 0.24.

tools.name: Regex Lab
tools.tagline: Live JavaScript regex tester with groups, replace, and a cheat sheet
tools.description: Zero-dependency regex tester: flag chips, live match highlighting, a table of every numbered and named capture group, replace preview with $1/$<name>/$& support, sample pattern/text pairs, and a collapsible cheat sheet. Guards against catastrophic backtracking with char/match caps and a time budget; state persists to localStorage.

tools.name: Hash Forge
tools.tagline: Hash and HMAC text with MD5 and the SHA family, fully client-side.
tools.description: Paste text and get MD5 plus SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512 digests, with optional HMAC output from a shared secret. MD5 is provided by vendored blueimp-md5 under MIT; SHA and HMAC use the browser Web Crypto API. No data leaves the page.

tools.name: JSONPath Lab
tools.tagline: Query JSON with JSONPath and inspect values, paths, and pointers.
tools.description: A two-pane JSONPath workbench: paste JSON, run paths such as $.items[*].name or filters, switch result types, and inspect matched values with path and JSON Pointer metadata. Powered by vendored jsonpath-plus under MIT and runs entirely in the browser.

tools.name: SQL Formatter
tools.tagline: Format SQL across common dialects with case and indentation controls.
tools.description: Client-side SQL formatting for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, BigQuery, Transact-SQL, Snowflake, Trino, and more. Choose dialect, keyword case, and indentation, then copy the formatted query. Uses vendored sql-formatter under MIT; queries stay local.

tools.name: Base Lab
tools.tagline: Encode and decode Base64, Base64URL, hex, binary, Base32, URLs, HTML, and JSON strings.
tools.description: Zero-dependency encoding workbench with UTF-8 correct Base64/Base64URL, RFC 4648 Base32, hex, binary, URL encoding, HTML entities, and JSON string escaping. Includes file mode, auto-detect decode, and copyable outputs; all processing stays in the browser.

tools.name: Epoch & Time
tools.tagline: Convert Unix epochs, ISO dates, local time, time zones, and durations.
tools.description: Zero-dependency time workbench built on native Intl: live clock, Unix seconds and milliseconds conversion, ISO/native date parsing, saved comparison time zones, and a duration calculator. Designed for debugging logs, tokens, and scheduled jobs without sending data anywhere.

tools.name: ID Forge
tools.tagline: Generate UUID v4, UUID v7, ULID, and nanoid identifiers in batches.
tools.description: Zero-dependency identifier workbench with UUID v4, UUID v7, ULID, monotonic ULID, and nanoid generation, batch copy/download, uppercase options, custom nanoid alphabet, and UUID/ULID decoding. Uses browser crypto only.

tools.name: Diff Lab
tools.tagline: Compare two text blocks with side-by-side line diffs and unified output.
tools.description: Zero-dependency text diff workbench using an in-browser LCS line comparison. Paste before and after text, inspect added/removed/unchanged lines, swap inputs, and copy a unified diff-style output.

tools.name: Color Lab
tools.tagline: Parse, convert, compare, and generate palettes across modern color formats.
tools.description: Zero-dependency color workbench: parse hex, rgb, hsl, and oklch values; list every format as copyable output; check WCAG contrast with pass badges; and generate OKLCH-perceptual palettes. sRGB to/from OKLCH conversion is implemented from scratch.

tools.name: Bytecode Lab
tools.tagline: Compile a tiny language into stack-VM bytecode and step through execution.
tools.description: Zero-dependency compiler pipeline lab: edit a small expression language, inspect tokens, AST, emitted bytecode, constants, labels, and stack-machine execution one instruction at a time. Built for understanding how source turns into compact VM instructions without sending code anywhere.

tools.name: WASM Explorer
tools.tagline: Assemble WAT, disassemble WASM, inspect bytes, and run simple exports.
tools.description: Client-side WebAssembly workbench powered by vendored WABT: assemble WAT into real WASM bytes, disassemble uploaded modules back to WAT, inspect imports, exports, sections, hex output, and run simple zero-argument exports. No code leaves the browser.

tools.name: Compression Lab
tools.tagline: Compare gzip, deflate, native streams, and Brotli over text or local files.
tools.description: Local-only compression benchmark and inspection bench: paste text or drop a file, compare fflate gzip/zlib/deflate, browser CompressionStream codecs, and Brotli WASM. Reports compressed size, ratio, timing, entropy estimate, and decompression verification.

tools.name: Text Toolkit
tools.tagline: Transform text with case tools, line ops, whitespace cleanup, extractors, and guarded regex replace.
tools.description: Zero-dependency swiss-army text transformer: paste a block and run case conversion, sort, dedupe, shuffle, trim, number, prefix, suffix, whitespace cleanup, slugify, wrap/unwrap, email/URL/number extraction, and guarded regex find/replace. Includes live stats, copy, download, and chain-back-to-input.

tools.name: Unit Converter
tools.tagline: Zero-dependency converter across 11 domains, offset-correct temperature and SI-vs-IEC data.
tools.description: A single-file converter across Length, Mass, Temperature, Volume, Area, Speed, Time, Data, Pressure, Energy and Angle. Pick a category, enter a value and unit, and get a live high-precision grid of every unit with per-value copy, plus a quick A↔B converter and a unit filter. Temperature uses proper offset formulas (C/F/K/R), and data units distinguish SI (MB) from IEC (MiB). Last category persists to localStorage.

tools.name: Number Base
tools.tagline: Convert bases, inspect bits, and run BigInt bitwise operations.
tools.description: Zero-dependency base and bitwise workbench: convert between binary, octal, decimal, hex, and any base 2-36; toggle bits on a 64-bit grid; run AND, OR, XOR, NOT, NAND, NOR, and shifts; inspect signed versus unsigned two's-complement values and endianness. Runs entirely in the browser on BigInt.

tools.name: URL Inspector
tools.tagline: Parse, edit, rebuild, and explain URLs with duplicate query parameter support.
tools.description: Zero-dependency URL parser and builder using native URL and URLSearchParams APIs. Break a URL into protocol, credentials, host, port, path, query, and hash; edit each part; rebuild the URL live; and manage query parameters in an editable table with duplicate-key support.

tools.name: String Escaper
tools.tagline: Escape and unescape JSON, JavaScript, HTML, URL, Base64, Unicode, CSV, and shell strings.
tools.description: Zero-dependency string escape workbench for hostile text: convert to and from JSON strings, JavaScript escapes, HTML entities, URL components, Base64 UTF-8, Unicode code points, CSV fields, and shell-safe single quotes. Includes byte counts, delta sizing, swap, copy, and sample data.

tools.name: Image Inspector
tools.tagline: Drop an image for dimensions, histogram, palette, EXIF, and resize — all in-browser
tools.description: Zero-dependency image analysis. Drop, pick, or paste an image to read dimensions, aspect ratio, size/type and megapixels; view an R/G/B/luminance histogram; extract a 6-swatch median-cut palette (hex + %) and average color; sample any pixel with a hover eyedropper; parse best-effort JPEG EXIF; and resize + re-encode to PNG/JPEG/WebP to see the new byte size. Nothing is uploaded.

tools.name: CIDR Calculator
tools.tagline: IPv4/IPv6 subnet workbench: analyze, subnet, aggregate, convert.
tools.description: Zero-dependency IP subnet workbench for IPv4 and IPv6. Analyze a CIDR (network, broadcast, first/last host, netmask, wildcard, host count) with a visual mask bit ruler; split a block into equal subnets or a target prefix; aggregate CIDR lists into minimal covering blocks; test IP membership; convert addresses between dotted/decimal/hex/binary and expanded/compressed IPv6 — all on BigInt, entirely in your browser.

tools.name: Passphrase Forge
tools.tagline: Diceware passphrases and random passwords with honest entropy math
tools.description: Zero-dependency, fully client-side secret generator. Builds Diceware passphrases from the complete EFF large wordlist (7,776 words) or random-character passwords with configurable sets, using crypto.getRandomValues and rejection sampling for unbiased selection. Shows exact entropy in bits, guess-space size, and an honest time-to-crack at 10^12 guesses/s. Batch up to 50. Nothing is transmitted, stored, or logged.

tools.name: Function Plotter
tools.tagline: Zero-dependency graphing calculator with a safe expression parser.
tools.description: Plot one or more expressions in x on a crisp, pannable, zoomable canvas. A hand-built shunting-yard parser evaluates + - * / ^ %, pi/e, and the usual functions (sin, cos, exp, log, sqrt, …) with no eval or Function on user input. Per-curve colors and toggles, hover crosshair readout, optional numeric derivative overlay, discontinuity handling, and PNG export.
